name: Cuevas Lituergo from Villanueva de la Reina
name_original: Cuevas Lituergo desde Villanueva de la Reina
region_intro: Hiking around Villanueva De La Reina offers diverse landscapes, from the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park to the fertile plains of the Guadalquivir River. The region is characterized by extensive olive groves, gentle hills, and Mediterranean forests of holm oaks and cork oaks. These varied environments provide a range of hiking opportunities for different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Villanueva De La Reina The most popular hiking route is Castillo de Burgalimar and Migaldías fort - circular via the Bronze trail, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores historical forts and offers scenic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cuevas Lituergo from Villanueva de la Reina, a moderate 8.4 miles (13.5 km) path. This trail leads from the town into the surrounding natural areas, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Local hikers also love the Tails of the Rumblar Reservoir – Baños de la Encina loop from Baños de la Encina, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail leading through reservoir landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Hiking around Villanueva De La Reina is defined by the rugged beauty of the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park, the tranquil Guadalquivir River, and the expansive olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging routes in the hills. The routes in Villanueva De La Reina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva De La Reina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Villanueva De La Reina?
A: There are over 40 hiking routes around Villanueva De La Reina, offering a diverse range of experiences. You'll find 16 easy routes perfect for a relaxed stroll and 27 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Villanueva De La Reina?
A: Hiking in Villanueva De La Reina offers a rich variety of landscapes. You can explore the Mediterranean forests of the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park, characterized by holm oaks and cork oaks, or enjoy the fertile plains along the Guadalquivir River. Vast olive groves and gentle rolling hills also define the scenic beauty of the region.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Villanueva De La Reina?
A: Yes, Villanueva De La Reina offers several easy routes suitable for families. These trails often feature gentle terrain and shorter distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38142668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tails of the Rumblar Reservoir – Baños de la Encina loop from Baños de la Encina&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.8 km path that takes less than two hours to complete, perfect for a family outing.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Villanueva De La Reina?
A: The best time to hike in Villanueva De La Reina is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summer can be very hot, especially in July and August, while winters are generally mild but can have occasional rain.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes around Villanueva De La Reina are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A notable circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981164708/castillo-de-burgalimar-and-migaldias-fort-circular-via-the-bronze-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Burgalimar and Migaldías fort - circular via the Bronze trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.3 km loop that explores historical sites and offers scenic views.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I find along the trails?
A: The hiking trails often lead to or pass by several interesting landmarks. You might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3144645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Baños de la Encina (Burgalimar Castle)&lt;/a&gt;, or natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2862464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinarejo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Rumblar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6700562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Cristo del Llano&lt;/a&gt; are also points of interest.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Villanueva De La Reina?
A: The trails in Villanueva De La Reina are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from the tranquil olive groves to the rugged beauty of the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park, and the well-maintained paths that offer a variety of experiences for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any trails that offer good viewpoints?
A: Yes, the region features several trails with excellent viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38142846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Rumblar Reservoir – Ermita del Cristo del Llano loop from Baños de la Encina&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route specifically highlighted for its scenic vistas. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1278398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pastores Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Can I hike along the Guadalquivir River?
A: Yes, the Guadalquivir River flows through the municipal district, creating fertile plains and offering pleasant walks along its banks. These riverside paths are often lined with orchards and olive trees, providing a different, more serene hiking experience compared to the mountainous areas.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The diverse ecosystems around Villanueva De La Reina support a wide array of wildlife. In the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park, you might spot deer, wild boars, and various bird species like the imperial eagle and griffon vulture. The park is also a stronghold for the elusive Iberian lynx. In the olive groves and riverine habitats, you could see rabbits, foxes, and various smaller birds.

Q: Are there any easy routes suitable for beginners?
A: Absolutely. There are 16 easy routes around Villanueva De La Reina, perfect for beginners or those looking for a less strenuous walk. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14622123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Facade of the Ducal Palace – Hermitage of Our Father Jesus loop from Bailén&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.1 km trail that takes about 1 hour 36 minutes to complete.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in Villanueva De La Reina?
A: Hiking durations vary significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. Easy routes, like the 6.8 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38142668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tails of the Rumblar Reservoir – Baños de la Encina loop&lt;/a&gt;, can take around 1 hour 52 minutes. Moderate trails, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987840368/cuevas-lituergo-from-villanueva-de-la-reina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas Lituergo from Villanueva de la Reina&lt;/a&gt; (13.5 km), typically require about 3 hours 33 minutes.
tips:
Caves dating from prehistoric times. It is said that they were inhabited in Roman times, sticking to the abundant remains of Roman pieces located in the area.
The caves have been inhabited throughout history, perhaps not constantly, but at intervals. Proof of this is the relatively good preservation of them. There is even a census of 183 people in 1945, collected in a census from Villanueva de la Reina.
